intent: Cross-version replay of rocky9-role-denial against el10 policyvers 35. RBAC/role constraint on process dyntransition (newrole_t, staff_r -> sysadm_r). Verifies Constraint bucket is stable for role-constraint scenarios across policy versions.

oracle.audit2why_detail: constrain process { transition noatsecure siginh rlimitinh dyntransition } ((r1 == r2 -Fail-) or (t1 == can_change_process_role) and (t2 == process_user_target -Fail-) or (t1 == cron_source_domain -Fail-) and (t2 == cron_job_domain -Fail-) or (t1 == can_system_change -Fail-) and (r2 == system_r -Fail-) or (t1 == process_uncond_exempt -Fail-)); Constraint DENIED. Possible cause is the source role (staff_r) and target role (sysadm_r) are different.

triage_expectation.should_decline_with_reason: RBAC/role constraint violation: source role staff_r differs from target role sysadm_r on process dyntransition. A TE allow cannot fix a role constraint.
